claims.claim: Adrien Yanz's speed will be too much for Cody Garbrandt.
claims.quote: I think Adrien Yanz, I think the speed could be a little too much for Cody Garbrand.

claims.claim: Cody Garbrandt could stifle Adrien Yanz if he utilizes his wrestling.
claims.quote: But if Cody could use his wrestling, he could stifle Adrien Yanz

claims.claim: Luke Riley will benefit from the energy and stardom associated with Paddy Pimblett.
claims.quote: I think Luke Riley getting a little taste of that stardom that Patty brings with him. So, I think that that that's going to be kind of an X-factor in this fight

claims.claim: Gable Steveson is a once-in-a-generation talent.
claims.quote: Gable Stefson's a once in a generation type of talent.

claims.claim: Robert Whitaker is physically strong and fast at light heavyweight.
claims.quote: he felt really, really strong. He was still really really fast. He was still very elusive.

claims.claim: Nikita Krillov's best attacks rely on linear blitzing combinations.
claims.quote: a lot of Nikita Krill's best attacks, they come on a straight line. He comes from a karate background. A lot of very linear blitzing combinations

claims.claim: Robert Whitaker utilizes lateral movement and stance changes to avoid linear attacks.
claims.quote: Robert Whitaker as we know a lot of lateral movement. definitely good with his footwork... look to see Robert Whitaker use the stance changes, the lateral footwork

claims.claim: Robert Whitaker effectively utilizes a 'double strike' combination of a cross followed by a kick.
claims.quote: Robert Whitaker does a very good job utilizing the double strike.

claims.claim: King Green is currently on a three-fight win streak against young prospects.
claims.quote: King Green's not doing like the veterans tour... he's out here taking out young guns.

claims.claim: King Green has a professional record of 35 wins and 17 losses.
claims.quote: Quite the record, 35 and 17

claims.claim: King Green is currently on a three-fight win streak.
claims.quote: as of late on a threeight win streak.

claims.claim: King Green's defensive style of holding his hands low makes him susceptible to being hit.
claims.quote: the problem is now is defensively I'm very susceptible.

claims.claim: Terrence McKini possesses Division I level wrestling ability.
claims.quote: He has got division one level wrestling

claims.claim: Terrence McKini has the ability to generate significant power in his punches without loading up or turning his hips.
claims.quote: he doesn't have to load up his punches a lot and he doesn't turn his hips. They're really a lot of arm punches, but he just has so much power that he's able to do that.

claims.claim: Bobby Green relies on establishing a rhythm and finding his range to be effective.
claims.quote: Bobby does his best work when he can get in a groove. Once he can establish his range... that's when he can really get going.

claims.claim: Terrence McKini's tendency to start fights at a high pace will prevent Bobby Green from finding his rhythm.
claims.quote: Terrence is just going to not give him a chance to do any of that. He's going to come out the gates hot. He's going to stun him.

claims.claim: Brandon Royval utilizes a long guard and jabbing feints as part of his fighting style.
claims.quote: you're going to get a lot of that long guard, a lot of those jabbing types of faints.

claims.claim: Bobby's fighting style relies on him finding his range and timing to get into a rhythm.
claims.quote: Bobby's game really revolves around him finding his range. Once he finds his range and he knows how much distance he has to cover to move in and out, that's when he can really get going.

claims.claim: Terrence McKenna will prevent Bobby from establishing his rhythm by coming out aggressively.
claims.quote: Terrence is just going to not give him a chance to do any of that. He's going to come out the gates hot.

claims.claim: Brandon Royval utilizes a 'long guard' and frequent feints as part of his striking style.
claims.quote: you're going to get a lot of that long guard, a lot of those jabbing types of faints.

claims.claim: Brandon Royval's jittery movement and high activity level force opponents to over-anticipate his strikes.
claims.quote: when he's so busy and he's so jittery, it makes you over anticipate uh him firing shots.

claims.claim: Lonnie Kavanaaugh has a tendency to drop his hands when exiting range.
claims.quote: Lonnie Kavanaaugh moved out of what he thought was his range with his hands literally completely at his waist.

claims.claim: Lonnie Kavanaaugh's loss to Charles Johnson served as a valuable learning experience regarding defensive responsibility.
claims.quote: the best thing to ever happen to him was that loss to Charles Johnson. ... The lesson was learned.

claims.claim: Lonnie Kavanaaugh successfully neutralized Brandon Moreno's power by attacking his lead leg with calf kicks.
claims.quote: The way he was able to just dissect Brandon Moreno with the calf kicks. ... you take away when you're fighting a guy that's really good with his hands, you got to attack the legs

claims.claim: Brandon Royval will likely switch stances against Lonnie Kavanaaugh to avoid calf kicks.
claims.quote: One thing I think Brandon Royal is going to do is he's going to change stances with Lonnie to try to stay in open stance to avoid the calf kicks.

claims.claim: Brandon Royval is more flat-footed than Brandon Moreno.
claims.quote: Brandon Royal, like I said, little more jittery with his legs, a lot more stutter step faints, and he will jump in and out of range with both of his feet at the same time to land punches so he can hit and not get hit to stick and move.

claims.claim: Brandon Royval will change stances to avoid calf kicks.
claims.quote: One thing I think Brandon Royal is going to do is he's going to change stances with Lonnie to try to stay in open stance to avoid the calf kicks.

claims.claim: Cory Sandhagen has only lost to the best fighters in the division.
claims.quote: he's only lost to the best guy. Alja Sterling, TJ Dillasha, Pod Jan, Umar Mega, Mor Rob Dval, Villi.

claims.claim: Cory Sandhagen suffered an elbow injury during his fight against Rob Font.
claims.quote: I think he had an elbow injury.

claims.claim: Mario Bautista is capable of fighting to his opponent's weaknesses rather than relying solely on his own strengths.
claims.quote: he's getting so good that even if he can fight you with his strengths, he'll he'll play to your weaknesses.

claims.claim: Mario Bautista has no weaknesses.
claims.quote: Mario Batista has no weakness.

claims.claim: Mario Bautista has the ability to fight opponents in their weakest areas rather than relying on his own strengths.
claims.quote: He doesn't have to play to his strengths. He can fa fight you with your weaknesses.

claims.claim: Mario Bautista has no weak parts to his game.
claims.quote: Mario Batista has no weakness. There's no weak part to his game.

claims.claim: Cory Sandhagen has plateaued in his development.
claims.quote: It's not like Cory Sanhagen's hit. Like, I feel like he's plateaued and he's done.

claims.claim: Patty Pimblett is physically enormous for the lightweight division.
claims.quote: Patty Pimpblelet, enormous for the weight class.

claims.claim: Patty Pimblett has an incredible chin and is extremely durable.
claims.quote: The guy's got an incredible chin. He's extremely durable.

claims.claim: Patty Pimblett is not defensively responsible.
claims.quote: Not the most defensively responsible guy.

claims.claim: Benoit Saint Denis was exposed for lacking a proper game plan in his loss to Dustin Poirier.
claims.quote: That was the fight where he got exposed for not having a head coach. Not that he got exposed for anything from a skill standpoint, but he just didn't have the right game plan in that fight.

claims.claim: Benoit Saint Denis has won four straight fights by finish since hiring a head coach.
claims.quote: since then four straight wins Kyle Propel, Mauricio, Benil Daruse, Dan Hooker, all by finish.

claims.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis relies on a style of walking opponents down on a straight line while shooting double-leg takedowns with little regard for submissions.
claims.quote: heavy southpaw striker, likes to just walk you down on a straight line, will shoot the double with complete disregard to getting his neck caught in any submissions.

claims.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis has high-level top pressure and positional awareness on the ground.
claims.quote: Once he gets you on the ground, he's so heavy on top. He's got heavy hips. He's got good positioning, good positional awareness on the ground

claims.claim: Benoit Saint-Denis's tendency to shoot takedowns on a straight line makes him vulnerable to Paddy Pimblett's guillotine and triangle chokes.
claims.quote: That's where Patty can jump for these tricky triangles. That's where Patty can use that guillotine to thwart, excuse me. That's where Patty Pimble can use the guillotines to thwart Benwis Saintani from getting takedowns

claims.claim: Paddy Pimblett's chin may be compromised due to the significant damage taken in his fight against Justin Gaethje less than seven months ago.
claims.quote: Is the chin compromised? You know, did he not take enough time off? That could play a factor in this fight.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor possesses the best check cross in UFC history.
claims.quote: The best check cross I have ever seen in the UFC. The best check cross I've ever seen in my life was actually the one against Jose Aldo.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor's five-year layoff makes it unlikely that he has evolved his fighting style.
claims.quote: it's hard for me to believe that this guy truly has evolved his style.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor has a wide variety of weapons including spinning attacks and hook kicks that he has not always utilized due to his reliance on boxing.
claims.quote: He's got spinning attacks. He's got snapping cra, you know, he's got he's got hook kicks, he's got snapping front kicks, he's got a wide variety of weapons that we we haven't always seen because he's primarily leaned on his boxing.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor has not truly evolved his style during his time away from the sport.
claims.quote: it's hard for me to believe that this guy truly has evolved his style.

claims.claim: Max Holloway is more active and focused on MMA than Conor McGregor.
claims.quote: Max Holloway since the time they fought until Now, his primary focus has been just mixed martial arts. Like, that's it's it's he's all in on the sport.

claims.claim: Max Holloway's reach is shorter than average for his size, which he compensates for by keeping his hands extended rather than close to his body.
claims.quote: That's how he kind of compensates for having a shorter reach is he doesn't fight from his with his hands here. He holds them out a little bit.

claims.claim: Max Holloway is difficult to take down compared to other fighters.
claims.quote: Max Holloway is not easy to take down.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor lacks the specific grip strength required to replicate Charles Oliveira's success in taking Max Holloway down.
claims.quote: Conor McGregor's strong, but he's not strong like Charles. That's why Charles was able to take Max down.

claims.claim: The 170-pound weight class favors Max Holloway because he has a larger frame that carries the weight better than Conor McGregor's.
claims.quote: I think that this fight being at 170 pounds favors him because I think that he has a bigger frame.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor's performance will suffer in later rounds due to the extra size at 170 pounds causing his punches to lose snap.
claims.quote: I think when the punches start to lose their snap, if this fight gets into the later rounds, everything is going to become a lot more labored.

claims.claim: Max Holloway did not take significant damage in his fight against Charles Oliveira.
claims.quote: he didn't take any damage in that fight

claims.claim: Max Holloway's frame is better suited to hold his current weight than Conor McGregor's.
claims.quote: his frame being able to hold this weight better than than Connors

claims.claim: Conor McGregor is returning to competition after a long layoff.
claims.quote: Connor coming back after a long layoff

claims.claim: Terrence McKinney is a bad stylistic matchup for Bobby Green at this stage in Green's career.
claims.quote: this is a bad stylistic matchup for Bobby Green at this stage in his career

claims.claim: Mario Bautista has evolved enough to defeat Cory Sandhagen.
claims.quote: he has the skills and he's evolved enough to go out there and pull off the upset over Cory Sanhagen

claims.claim: Conor McGregor possesses significant power as an X factor in the fight.
claims.quote: the X factor is Connor's power
